key_insight: A hybrid GNN-LSTM surrogate can learn how local short-fiber microstructure and loading history control the static stress-strain response of additively manufactured thermoplastic composites.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast prediction of stiffness and damage-prone regions in SFT parts, supporting design screening and digital-twin workflows without repeated costly finite-element microstructure simulations.
method: Reconstruct microstructures from micro-CT data and partition them into Voronoi-based fiber-interaction cells.
Homogenize each cell with nonlinear FE simulations that include matrix damage to generate training targets.
Train a GNN-LSTM to encode microstructural topology and history-dependent mechanical evolution.
evidence_signal: The surrogate matches high-fidelity FE stress-strain predictions with about R^2 ≈ 0.98 and reduces computation by more than two orders of magnitude.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of micro-CT reconstruction, calibrated damage laws, and whether the training microstructures cover the full manufacturing variability of target parts.
paper_title: On Surrogate Modeling of Static Response of AM Short-Fiber Thermoplastics Using Graph Neural Networks
